diagrams.title: Thread vs Process Structure
diagrams.description: Comparison showing that processes are isolated containers with separate address spaces, while threads share the process address space
elements: Process 2: Text, Data, Heap, Stack (Thread 1, Thread 2)
Shared resources: Files, Signals, Memory between threads in same process
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental difference between processes and threads and why threads are lightweight

diagrams.title: Threading Models Comparison
diagrams.description: Three threading models showing how user threads map to kernel threads in different architectures
elements: Many-to-One: Multiple User Threads -> Single Kernel Thread
One-to-One: Each User Thread -> Each Kernel Thread
Many-to-Many: Multiple User Threads -> Multiple Kernel Threads (with multiplexer)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how different OS architectures handle thread mapping and understanding trade-offs between efficiency and parallelism

diagrams.title: Thread Life Cycle State Diagram
diagrams.description: State diagram showing the five main states of a thread and transitions between them
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how threads move through different states during execution and what triggers each transition
